blockingIssues.description: SOLID bridge drops compat-visible `normal` and `thickness`, so payload fidelity is not preserved through native/compat/native roundtrip.
Face3D bridge cannot preserve compat `invisible_edges`, and coverage misses this observable payload regression.
SHAPE bridge only preserves insertion, size, and shape number while dropping other compat-visible payload fields such as name, rotation/scaling, normal, thickness, and style metadata.
PolygonMesh and PolyfaceMesh bridge-back collapses mesh dimensions/face records into flat vertex lists, so family semantics are not preserved.
HATCH bridge-back panics on spline boundary edges instead of preserving or safely accounting for valid compat hatch content.

Recorded feature commit `11579d20f655a7713823cf86ca37770006efc7f3` only updates `.factory/services.yaml` and does not contain the claimed bridge implementation/tests, so the feature lacks attributable completed-change evidence.
WIPEOUT reverse bridge rebuilds local clip vertices from world-space boundary vertices, corrupting wipeout geometry on native/compat/native roundtrip.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the `dxf-bridge-worker` skill to use the working bridge test command `cargo test --bin H7CAD native_bridge -- --test-threads=16` instead of `cargo test --lib native_bridge`, and note that workers should verify stale manifest commands before using them.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All four review reports flagged the stale bridge test command as a repeated issue; the worker transcripts had to deviate because the root package has no library target.

rejectedObservations.observation: Update `.factory/services.yaml` with the working bridge test command.
rejectedObservations.reason: already applied in the repository state reviewed by scrutiny

rejectedObservations.observation: Add AGENTS guidance to require payload-level assertions for WIPEOUT-like entities.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; the existing AGENTS and skill guidance already require field-level assertions, and the concrete gap is reviewer/feature execution rather than missing mission guidance
